data.content_ref: inline:The claim requires critic precision on wrong candidates > 0.5, but the measured hard-set data (critic precision 0.125 at recall 1.0, net fixed-minus-broke 0) refutes this as a necessary condition for the strict-benefit zone. The mechanism's own net-benefit formula (recall_wrong − falseflag_rate_correct) × P(fragile-correct) contains no precision term at all; a high-recall, low-precision critic can still yield net benefit if falseflag_rate_correct is low enough — the data shows recall=1.0 with precision=0.125, and the only reason net was zero was that 4/5 majorities were already right (e too low), not because precision was insufficient. Imposing precision > 0.5 as a hard gate contradicts the repository's own evidence and the target's own formula.
